author: crabhunter
post:
$FRMM 

The beauty of the markets, is you don’t pay interest on the money you secure from it; unless you’re locked into dividends.  

So if they’re averaging 10% ( sorry not 11% as other msg) you can add in a savings from traditional bank/institutional borrowing vehicles, which for emerging models and endeavors can be steep.  And toxic. 

So personally, I’d give those margins another  7%. So to me, they’re returning 17% on equity.  Less sustainment costs for the assets, like insurance on the jet engines.

author: crabhunter
post:
$FRMM 

Bionicman…like the handle. 

But if tokenized jet engines “sounds” like a scam…you near some bionic ears.  

More like Lindsay Austin, the bionic woman. 

Forum owns them, and leases out at $90K each/month.  Called recurring revenue. 

What I’m not aware of is their amortization period. Believed this is what they used the equity from offerings to purchase. 

And if you read the shareholder report, it indicates 11% profit margin. Not bad.

author: BottomFisher_
post:
Weekend watchlist mode on. 
 
This is how I scan for potential setups — not chasing noise, just mapping momentum + sentiment shifts. 
 
Featured names on radar: 
$FRMM 
$ICG 
$MATH 
$ABLV 
 
Macro tape backdrop: 
Futures pointing higher  
Oil futures sliding ~-8% 
 
Meanwhile, Nasdaq is on a 13-session win streak — longest since 1992. That kind of trend doesn’t happen in isolation; it builds liquidity rotation underneath. 
 
A lot of traders call this “late cycle”… others call it opportunity. 
 
Either way, the same question always hits: 
Was this a dip… or the dip of the cycle? 
 
What did you step into while fear was still loud?

author: Lowkeyoptions
post:
$FRMM 

The announcement gives Forum a more explicit response to the gap it says exists between its market value and what the board sees as the intrinsic value of the business. 

The company, which describes itself as a digital-asset platform focused on tokenized real-world assets, said the repurchase program was reactivated effective April 15 and could include buybacks in volumes that exceed the normal limits of the SEC’s Rule 10b-18 safe harbor. Forum said the board views repurchases at current price levels as a direct expression of confidence in the business.
